channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Hacker News] Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
item.description: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models

item.title: [Hacker News] Siri AI
item.description: Siri AI

item.title: [Anthropic] Paving the way for agents in biology
item.description: Paving the way for agents in biology

item.title: [Hacker News] Massachusetts bans sale of precise location data in new privacy rights bill
item.description: Massachusetts bans sale of precise location data in new privacy rights bill

item.title: [Hacker News] AI Is Slowing Down
item.description: AI Is Slowing Down

item.title: [Hacker News] xAI is looking more like a datacentre REIT than a frontier lab
item.description: xAI is looking more like a datacentre REIT than a frontier lab

item.title: [GitHub Trending] google/skills — Agent Skills for Google products and technologies
item.description: Google released agent skills framework for integrating Google products and technologies.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] phuryn/pm-skills — PM Skills Marketplace: 100+ agentic skills, commands, and plugins — from discovery to strategy, execution, launch, and growth.
item.description: GitHub project provides 100+ agentic skills and plugins for product management tasks across discovery, strategy, execution, and growth.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] Andyyyy64/whichllm — Find the local LLM that actually runs and performs best on your hardware. Ranked by real, recency-aware benchmarks, not parameter count. One command, run it instantly.
item.description: whichllm ranks local LLMs by actual performance benchmarks and recency rather than parameter count.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/luongnv89 — A visual, example-driven guide to Claude Code — from basic concepts to advanced agents, with copy-paste templates that bring immediate value.
item.description: A visual guide for Claude Code covers basic concepts through advanced agents with copy-paste templates.

item.title: [Hacker News] Replies to comments on my "LLMs are eroding my career" post
item.description: Replies to comments on my "LLMs are eroding my career" post

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Elmes*: Automated Construction of Fine-Grained Evaluation Rubrics for Large Language Models in Long-Tail Educational Scenarios
item.description: Elmes* automates creation of fine-grained educational evaluation rubrics for LLMs, revealing multidimensional teaching capabilities across 330 scenarios in Edu-330 dataset.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FAIR-Calib: Frontier-Aware Instability-Reweighted Calibration for Post-Training Quantization of Diffusion Large Language Models
item.description: Study presents FAIR-Calib for quantizing diffusion language models, using reweighted calibration to protect fragile decisions at token-writing frontiers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Multi-Scale Feature Attention Network for Polymer Classification using THz Dual-Comb Spectroscopy
item.description: MSFAN deep learning model classifies 12 polymer types with 85.2% accuracy using terahertz dual-comb spectroscopy data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MacArena: Benchmarking Computer Use Agents on an Online macOS Environment
item.description: MacArena benchmark of 421 macOS tasks reveals platform presents harder GUI challenges than Linux benchmarks for computer-use agents.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] WAV: Multi-Resolution Block Residual Routing for Deep Decoder-Only Transformers
item.description: WAV introduces multi-resolution block residual routing for deep transformers using directional detail bases, achieving better performance on language modeling at 48 layers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Are you sure? A Comprehensive and Comprehensible Survey of Uncertainty Quantification in Symbolic Regression
item.description: Survey reviews uncertainty quantification methods in symbolic regression across frequentist, Bayesian, and model selection approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Generative Models Erode Human Temporal Learning Through Market Selection
item.description: Generative models displace learning-intensive human work when verification costs exceed economic benefits across multiple fields.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Skip a Layer or Loop It? Learning Program-of-Layers in LLMs
item.description: Research demonstrates LLMs can dynamically skip or repeat layers per input, achieving equal or better accuracy with reduced layer execution.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Gaussian Process Latent Factor Regression for Low-Data, High-Dimensional Output Problems
item.description: Gaussian Process Latent Factor Regression enables predicting high-dimensional outputs from limited training data by integrating compression and prediction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Principles and Practice of Deep Representation Learning: or a Mathematical Theory of Memory
item.description: Book presents mathematical theory of representation learning to explain and design interpretable deep neural networks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Identity Trap in EEG Foundation Models: A Diagnostic Audit
item.description: Study finds EEG foundation models suffer from "Identity Trap" where subject-identity features dominate clinical signals, but can be corrected.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Capturing non-Markovian dynamics in non-equilibrium stochastic systems using flow matching
item.description: Flow matching method captures non-Markovian dynamics in stochastic particle systems, outperforming standard hydrodynamic models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Explainable Runtime Dependency Tracking for AI-RAN Conflict Monitoring
item.description: Researchers propose a Boolean matrix method to track parameter-KPI dependencies in AI-integrated Radio Access Networks for runtime conflict monitoring.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Uncertainty-Aware LLM-Guided Policy Shaping for Sparse-Reward Reinforcement Learning
item.description: LLM-guided policy shaping using uncertainty estimates improves reinforcement learning in sparse-reward environments.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Spatiotemporal Imputation with Graph-Informed Flow Matching
item.description: GiFlow framework improves spatiotemporal imputation using graph-informed priors and spatiotemporal vector fields instead of diffusion-based methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Towards Serverless Semi-Decentralized Federated Learning with Heterogeneous Optimizers
item.description: New federated learning methodology eliminates persistent server requirements by enabling cluster formation through device-to-device initialization with heterogeneous optimizers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Geography of Algorithmic Judgment: LLM Intermediaries, Place Identity, and Racial Steering in Housing Search
item.description: Behavioral audit finds LLMs demonstrate racial steering in housing search, with variation based on user identity and preferences.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] RECAP: Regression Evaluation for Continual Adaptation of Prompts
item.description: RECAP benchmark measures prompt optimization methods on evolving constraints with no test data visibility, finding current methods inadequate for production.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ShallowBench: Benchmarking Generative Drug Design Models on Shallow-Pocket Targets
item.description: ShallowBench benchmark demonstrates generative drug design models struggle with shallow-pocket targets like KRAS and MYC, indicating need for new architectural approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MSAIC-Net: A Multi-Scale Attention and Imbalance-Aware Contrastive Network for ECG-Based Myocardial Substrate Abnormality Detection
item.description: MSAIC-Net uses multi-scale attention and contrastive learning to improve ECG-based detection of myocardial abnormalities with better interpretability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Flatland: The Adventures of Gradient Descent with Large Step Sizes
item.description: Adaptive gradient descent methods achieve provably large step sizes by maintaining edge-of-stability conditions throughout neural network training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Synthics: Synthetic Physics-like Datasets for Machine Learning
item.description: Synthics generates synthetic regression datasets that structurally mirror physics equations using Bayesian probabilistic grammars, validated against real equation corpora.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TorchKM: A GPU-Oriented Library for Kernel Learning and Model Selection
item.description: TorchKM is a GPU-accelerated open-source library for kernel machines with scikit-learn-style API, offering speedups over standard baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Learn to Match: Two-Sided Matching with Temporally Extended Feedback
item.description: Learn2Match is a multi-agent reinforcement learning benchmark for dynamic two-sided matching markets with temporally extended feedback.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Performance Variation in Deep Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Study proposes percentile-based metrics to measure and analyze run-to-run performance variation in deep reinforcement learning algorithms.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Rolling-Window Framework for Churn Prediction and Behavioral Driver Identification
item.description: Rolling-window framework for churn prediction achieves 87.6% accuracy in non-contractual service environments using integrated feature and sequence-based learning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Federated Foundation Models over Vehicular Networks
item.description: Paper presents federated multi-modal foundation models for vehicular networks, discussing training principles, use cases, deployment challenges, and validation on Waymo data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Interpreting Learning Under Competing Models: Joint and Stepwise Approaches for Dynamic Cognitive Diagnosis
item.description: Joint analysis of skill structure and learning provides more reliable conclusions about student mastery progression than stepwise approaches when structures are uncertain.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SCALE: Scalable Cross-Attention Learning with Extrapolation for Agentic Workflow Scheduling
item.description: SCALE, a deep reinforcement learning scheduler, enables agentic LLM workflow scheduling across different cluster sizes without retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PandaAI: A Practical Agent CQ2 for Neuro-symbolic Data Analysis And Integrated Decision-Making in Quantitative Finance
item.description: PandaAI, a neuro-symbolic LLM agent, achieves 18.2% higher Rank IC and 25.7% lower drawdown in quantitative financial decision-making.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Improving Cross-Lingual Factual Recall via Consistency-Driven Reinforcement Learning
item.description: GRPO outperforms other methods for improving cross-lingual factual recall in large language models using a new 100K-fact multilingual dataset.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Re-Centering Humans in LLM Personalization
item.description: Study reveals LLMs personalize poorly with real data; models struggle to extract attributes and match human quality judgments.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] UnpredictaBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Distributional Randomness in LLMs
item.description: UnpredictaBench evaluates LLMs' ability to sample from target distributions using 448 problems and a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test-based metric.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] How Language Models Fail: Token-Level Signatures of Committed and Persistent Reasoning Failures
item.description: Researchers identify two reasoning failure modes in language models—committed failure and persistent uncertainty—using token-level uncertainty signals across 23 configurations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CAF-Gen: A Multi-Agent System for Enriching Argumentation Structures
item.description: CAF-Gen uses multi-agent feedback loops to enrich argument structures into formally complex CAF-compliant models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Piggyback Hypothesis of Generalization: Explaining and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment
item.description: Study identifies prefix tokens as cause of emergent misalignment in finetuned LLMs; proposes Token-Regularized Finetuning to mitigate.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] What Do People Actually Want From AI? Mapping Preference Plurality
item.description: Study of 1,500 global responses reveals people want divergent AI capabilities; even truthfulness definitions differ, exposing alignment method limitations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] HKJudge: A Legal Discourse-Annotated Corpus for Interpreting What Courts Find, How They Reason, and What They Rule
item.description: HKJudge provides the first sentence-level annotated corpus of Hong Kong court judgments to enable legal discourse analysis and judgment prediction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Signal-Driven Observation for Long-Horizon Web Agents
item.description: Signal-Driven Observation reduces context degradation in long-horizon web agents by returning only task-relevant DOM elements, triggered only when URLs change or interactions fail.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Data-Efficient Autoregressive-to-Diffusion Language Models via On-Policy Distillation
item.description: On-Policy Diffusion Language Models use knowledge distillation from frozen autoregressive teachers to convert them to diffusion models with 15x-7,000x fewer training tokens.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Does Topic Sentiment Cause Perceived Ideology? Comparing Human and LLM Annotations in Political News Articles
item.description: Fine-tuned GPT-4o-mini exhibits shortcut learning, falsely coupling sentiment to ideology in political news annotations, unlike human annotators.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Modular Monolingual Adaptation using Pretrained Language Models
item.description: Modular approach replacing tokenizers and freezing embeddings while tuning remaining parameters improves pretrained language model adaptation for low-resource languages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When to Think Deeply: Inhibitory Deliberation for LLM Reasoning
item.description: IDPR framework selectively invokes slow LLM reasoning by conditioning on fast-answer characteristics, improving accuracy while reducing computational cost.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Evidence Graph Consistency in Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Model-Dependent Analysis of Hallucination Detection
item.description: Evidence graph consistency detects hallucinations in Llama-2 but exhibits reversed diagnostic patterns in GPT-4, GPT-3.5, and Mistral models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PromptPrint: Behavioral Biometrics Through Natural Language Prompting in LLMs
item.description: Large study demonstrates LLM prompts form behavioral biometrics based on lexical patterns, vulnerable to semantic attacks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Four-Condition Diagnostic Protocol for Evidence Utilization in Long-Context and Retrieval-Augmented Language Models
item.description: Researchers propose a diagnostic protocol to test whether long-context and retrieval-augmented language models actually use provided evidence, finding task-dependent utilization failures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Better Codebooks Are Not Enough: Predictive Performance and Behavioral Reliability in LLM Political Event Coding
item.description: LLMs using refined codebooks achieve higher accuracy in political event coding but lack behavioral reliability under controlled changes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Explain Like I'm 5 or Whatever I Choose: Evaluating the Interactive Potential of Language Model Responses
item.description: Evaluation framework tests LLMs' ability to adjust language complexity across responses; most models show inconsistent results, with best model succeeding only 46% of time.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] TA-RAG: Tone-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Peer-Support Health Communication
item.description: TA-RAG framework applies prompt-based tone control to RAG systems for stigma-free, empathetic peer-support health communication without model fine-tuning.
